data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "A massive volcanic eruption (likely Thera) around 1200 BC caused a short‑term climatic cooling, leading to successive harvest failures across the Eastern Mediterranean.", "mechanism": "environmental stress via volcanic winter", "scope": {"covers": ["Mycenaean Greece", "Hittite Anatolia", "Ugarit", "Levantine city‑states"], "excludes": ["Egypt"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "High‑resolution ice‑core and dendrochronological records show no significant temperature drop in 1200 BC", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "Sequence: eruption → ash fallout → reduced solar insolation → crop failures → societal strain → collapse."}
